title: Cat Breathing Failure After Insect Sting: Emergency Triage Guide
meta_desc: If your cat is struggling to breathe after an insect sting, it is a life-threatening emergency. Learn about anaphylaxis and immediate action steps.
content_html:
What is Respiratory Failure After an Insect Sting?

When a cat is stung by an insect such as a bee, wasp, or hornet, their immune system may experience an extreme overreaction to the venom. This condition, known as anaphylaxis, causes the airways to swell and constrict. As the throat and lungs become compromised, the cat enters respiratory failure, meaning they can no longer transport sufficient oxygen to their brain and vital organs.

Is This an Emergency?

Urgency Level: High (Critical Emergency). Yes, breathing failure is a life-threatening situation. If your cat is gasping for air, wheezing, exhibiting open-mouth breathing, or has blue-tinged gums, they are in anaphylactic shock. This condition can progress to cardiac arrest within minutes. You must transport your cat to the nearest emergency veterinary hospital immediately.

Why a Photo Helps Triage

If it is safe to do so without delaying your departure, taking a quick photo or a five-second video of your cat's breathing and the sting site can be vital. A visual record allows the veterinary team to witness the exact nature of the respiratory distress and the severity of any localized swelling before the cat is even moved into the exam room. This information helps the clinical team prepare the necessary life-saving equipment, such as oxygen or epinephrine, before you even walk through the door.

faqs.question: Can I give my cat Benadryl for a sting?
faqs.answer: Do not attempt to give oral medication to a cat experiencing breathing failure, as they may aspirate it into their lungs. While antihistamines help minor reactions, anaphylaxis requires professional medical intervention like injectable epinephrine and oxygen therapy.

faqs.question: What are the signs of anaphylactic shock in cats?
faqs.answer: Beyond breathing difficulty, signs include sudden onset of vomiting, diarrhea, cold limbs, pale gums, collapse, or extreme lethargy immediately following a sting.

faqs.question: How quickly do symptoms of a sting reaction appear?
faqs.answer: Severe allergic reactions usually occur within minutes, though some symptoms may develop up to an hour after the initial insect sting.
